name: The Echo Park Time Travel Mart
description: Non-profit time travel mart sells goods you would need for a trip through the fourth dimension.

name: The Last Bookstore
description: This iconic L.A. bookshop is housed in an abandoned bank—both symbolic and chic.

name: The Museum of Death
description: World's largest collection of serial killer artwork and other macabre exhibits.

name: Sunken City
description: Remants of foundations, streets, and streetcar tracks inhabit the cliffs where a landslide occurred in 1929.

name: Bradbury Building
description: The legendary building that needed ghostly approval before being built.

name: Griffith Observatory's Tesla Coil
description: High on a hill overlooking Los Angeles lives a world famous, high power conical Tesla Coil.

name: Old Zoo Picnic Area
description: The abandoned zoo enclosures offer a perfect place to experience the other side of the bars.

name: Bronson Cave
description: It should come as no surprise that the best view of the Hollywood sign is from Batman's lair.

name: Hollywood Forever Cemetery
description: Resting place of Hollywood's immortals is also site of summer film screenings.

name: Galco's Soda Pop Stop
description: Shop selling 500 flavors of soda has something for everyone.

name: Garden of Oz
description: Hidden in the hills of Hollywood, this secluded Oz-themed garden is dotted with dozens of thrones.

name: Phantasma Gloria
description: One man's homage to the sun continues to grow like a glass fractal.

name: Watts Towers
description: America's most famous piece of self-built architecture.

name: Hollywood Sign
description: Hollywood's most famous landmark, the "temporary" sign that has stood for decades longer than intended.

name: Ennis House
description: This weird abode has inspired everything from the "House on Haunted Hill" to a robot hunter's pad.

name: Mosaic Tile House
description: Rainbow-hued local gem in Venice.

name: No Vacancy
description: A prohibition-style speakeasy takes up residence in the last Victorian residence in Hollywood.

name: Tiki-Ti
description: Established in the '60s, a family-owned tropical drink bar founded by Ray Buhen, bartender to the stars.

name: Philosophical Research Society
description: A library of rare occult books in Los Angeles.

name: La Brea Tar Pits Dragonfly Fossils
description: These delicate buggers are some of the rarest fossils that have bubbled up from the Tar Pits.

name: Cathedral of Our Lady of the Angels
description: Crypt mausoleum in cathedral's basement contains relics of third century Roman martyr, Saint Vibiana.

name: Pink's Hot Dogs
description: World-famous, Pink's still manages to keep its prized cult fetish status.

name: Linda Vista Hospital
description: A hospital closed seemingly in the middle of operations, leaving behind fodder for horror films and ghost hunters.

name: ‘Urban Light’
description: Scores of vintage street lamps shine along L.A.’s Miracle Mile.

name: Psychiatry: An Industry of Death Museum
description: A look into the Church of Scientology's deeply unfavorable view of psychiatry.

name: Valley Relics Museum
description: One man's vast collection of artifacts and ephemera from matchbooks and postcards to vintage neon signs and cars.

name: Venice of America Canals
description: After starting as a whimsical tourist attraction that eventually found its way to ruin, these renovated canals offer a lush and exotic locale on the Pacific Coast.

name: Los Feliz Murder Mansion
description: Left untouched since a double murder-suicide in the 1960s, Los Feliz murder mansion remains a neighborhood mystery.

name: The Magic Castle
description: The private clubhouse for the members of the Academy of Magical Arts.

name: Lummis Home ("El Alisal")
description: A castle that was built stone by stone by an eccentric journalist.

name: Ferndell Nature Museum
description: A common entry point to the Griffith Observatory trail holds its own wonders.

name: Screen Novelties
description: This Los Angeles animation studio is keeping the wonder of stop motion puppetry alive.

name: The Mar Vista Time Travel Mart
description: Sister store to the Echo Park Time Travel Store.

name: Velaslavasay Panorama
description: Panorama exhibition hall in old silent theater with a beautiful backyard garden.

name: St. Vincent Court
description: A faux European lane smack dab in downtown L.A.

name: Angels Flight
description: Historic funicular railway, believed to be the shortest railroad in the world.

name: Self-Realization Fellowship Lake Shrine
description: This serene oasis in an affluent Los Angeles neighborhood holds some of Gandhi's ashes and got Elvis into Eastern Philosophy.

name: Peace Awareness Labyrinth and Gardens
description: A spiritual retreat in the heart of Los Angeles offers stunning gardens and a labyrinth to lose yourself in.

name: The El Segundo Butterfly Preserve
description: A ghost town at the airport is now a refuge for blue butterflies.

name: The Masque
description: The remains of the first punk club in L.A.

name: Rosenheim Mansion
description: This 'Murder House' grand mansion has starred in everything from Bones to Buffy.

name: John Sowden House
description: Most famous work of one of Frank Lloyd Wright's sons.

name: Marilyn Monroe's Crypt
description: Her final resting place was intended to be modest and private, but the power of her popularity continues to draw visitors.

name: Halloween House
description: Everyone remembers Laurie Strode from Halloween, but this is the house where most of the blood was shed.

name: Idle Hour
description: The programmatic glory of LA's giant whiskey barrel building has become an upscale reminder of the city's kitschy roadside past.

name: Stay On Main Hotel
description: This brightly made over hotel hides decades of suicide, serial killers, and mysterious deaths.

name: Bob Baker Marionette Theater
description: Historic marionette theater features thousands of handmade puppets and free cups of ice cream.

name: Heritage Square Museum
description: Beautiful Victorian buildings from L.A.'s forgotten architectural past.

name: Hollywood Tower Apartments
description: This glamorous old building is said to have inspired the Tower of Terror.

name: Binoculars Building
description: In a city filled with Frank Gehry buildings, one stands out as one of the architect's weirder works.

name: Space Shuttle Endeavour
description: The final resting place of a space program monolith.

name: Musso and Frank Grill
description: This star-studded Old Hollywood restaurant is where fettuccine Alfredo made its United States debut.

name: Snow White Cottages
description: Magical-looking cottages that inspired one of the most famous animated films ever.

name: Chemosphere
description: This ultra-modern home clings to the slope of the Hollywood Hills on a precarious stilt.

name: Soap Plant / Wacko
description: An expansive book store, art gallery, and curio shop with wares ranging from tarot cards to novelty socks.

name: Japanese Garden at the Donald C. Tillman Water Reclamation Plant
description: This traditional garden in the San Fernando Valley doubled for Star Fleet Academy, and is irrigated entirely by wastewater.

name: Chicken Boy
description: The so-called "Statue of Liberty of Los Angeles" is on a one mutant mission to keep L.A. weird.

name: Los Angeles Science Fantasy Society
description: World's oldest operating science fiction and fantasy club, once home to Ray Bradbury and L. Ron Hubbard.

name: Thompson Home
description: Nancy Thompson's Home from "A Nightmare on Elm Street."

name: Holyland Exhibition
description: Small museum packed with artifacts recovered by the man rumored to be the real-life inspiration for Indiana Jones.

name: Feral Cat Colonies of Hollywood Forever Cemetery
description: Dozens of feral felines rule a segment of Hollywood's only graveyard.

name: Los Angeles Zoo Botanical Gardens
description: The L.A. Zoo has a secret garden of rare bootleg cycads, a palm specimen dating back 280 million years.

name: Barnsdall Art Park
description: This hilltop park offers magnificent views and a Frank Lloyd Wright masterpiece.

name: Museum of Tolerance
description: A multimedia experience that chronicles the atrocities of racism.

name: Japanese Cultural Village
description: A fashion designer's dazzling world of Japanese collectibles is hidden in a warehouse downtown.

name: Templo Santa Muerte
description: A shrine to a skeleton saint in the city of angels.

name: Wat Thai Temple Food Market
description: Every weekend, the parking lot of this Buddhist temple turns into a Thai street-food paradise.

name: The Adventurers' Club of Los Angeles
description: Hidden above a drug store on a nondescript corner of Lincoln Heights is a private clubhouse for intrepid explorers.

name: Box Canyon
description: The quiet canyon has a tumultuous history involving a doomsday cult.

name: Rustic Canyon's Murphy Ranch
description: World War II-era Nazi compound nestled in the Santa Monica hills.

name: Townhouse Bar and Speakeasy
description: From pre-war whiskey cocktails to prohibition-era speakeasy secrecy, this bar has seen it all.

name: The Tonga Hut
description: Though tiki culture has gone in and out of fashion, the Tonga Hut has always been flying the flag in Los Angeles.

name: Mildred E. Mathias Botanical Garden
description: This “living museum” at UCLA exhibits thousands of plant species from all over the world.

name: Two Bit Circus Micro-Amusement Park
description: A beloved carnival gets a modern makeover in Los Angeles.

name: Highland Park Bowl
description: L.A.'s oldest operating bowling alley is a beautifully restored homage to its speakeasy days.

name: Petersen Automotive Vault
description: Once strictly off-limits, this lower level housing curious and priceless vehicles of the Petersen Automotive Museum is now open to the public.

name: Miniature Garden of Whimsy
description: A quirky collection of figurines backdropped by city views is hidden next to a popular restaurant in the Hollywood hills.

name: Samuel Freeman House
description: This forgotten Frank Lloyd Wright house is an architectural marvel at the end of a sleepy residential street.

name: Hollywood Heritage Museum
description: A little barn in the middle of the city preserving Hollywood treasures since 1985.

name: Janis Joplin’s Hotel Room
description: The hotel room where Janis Joplin tragically died has now become a makeshift shrine to the singer.

name: Walt Disney's Carolwood Barn
description: The famous animator's private station house and man-cave was the birthplace of imagineering.

name: The Baked Potato Jazz Club
description: From sax to spuds, get 'em all at this landmark music establishment.

name: The Houdini Estate
description: A master of deception, Harry Houdini's greatest trick may have been living in two places at once.

name: Hollywood Sculpture Garden
description: This collector's home is a constant work in progress.

name: Evergreen Memorial Park & Crematory
description: Los Angeles's oldest cemetery allows visitors to trace the history of this proud city.

name: El Bordello Alexandra Apartments
description: An increasingly eccentric apartment building, topped with winged beasties.

name: Millennium Biltmore Hotel
description: This opulent hotel has been visited by Spider-Man, Charlie's Angels, and the Ghostbusters.

name: Korean Bell of Friendship
description: This symbol of peace is appropriately positioned overlooking the Pacific Ocean.

name: Global Effects, Inc.
description: Where the line between entertainment and aerospace blurs.

name: Venice Beach House
description: Two men, two visionaries of Venice Beach, one little hotel that smells like cookies.

name: The Music Box Steps
description: The site of a classic series of Laurel and Hardy pratfalls.

name: Old Warner Bros. Theatre Inside Downtown Jewelry Exchange
description: The beautiful 1920 Pantages Theatre remains hidden just underneath the LA Downtown Jewelry Exchange.

name: The Moore Lab of Zoology
description: One of the world's largest collections of rare and magnificent bird specimens.

name: Tom Mankiewicz Conservation Carousel
description: A carousel trades in its classic horses and calliope for endangered species, native flora and fauna, and a modern soundtrack.

name: Egasse-Braasch House
description: The house where Good Will Hunting was written is a surreal fairytale abode.

name: Great Wall of Los Angeles
description: One of the world's longest murals depicts the history of diversity in Los Angeles.

name: UCLA Meteorite Collection
description: What began with a single, 357-pound chunk of the Canyon Diablo meteorite grew to be an impressive collection.

name: Candy Cane Lane
description: Come crisp December nights, this fairly plain neighborhood becomes a holiday dream world.

name: Historic Street Lighting Museum
description: The illuminating collection of L.A. street lights is run by the city and only opens one day per month.

name: Riverside Roundabout
description: An L.A. traffic island where the faces of locals have been carved into massive granite eggs.

name: The Hollywood Roosevelt Hotel
description: Hollywood's oldest hotel is the site of the first Academy Awards, the birthplace of Marilyn Monroe's career, and the home of Montgomery Clift's ghost.

name: APEX Electronics
description: This stockpile of junked electronic and mechanical gadgets is a favorite among Hollywood prop masters.

name: Toto Canine Movie Star Memorial Marker
description: Because there's no place like home.

name: Brown Derby
description: Outposts of this restaurant chain were shaped like jaunty chapeaus.

name: The Bonaventure Hotel
description: The space-age building is home to one of the few remaining rotating restaurants in America, and the only one that inspired a 1980s sitcom.

name: Helios House
description: First LEED-certified gas station in the United States.

name: Elliott Smith's Tribute Wall
description: Pay your respects to a folk-punk legend.

name: Self-Realization Fellowship Meditation Gardens
description: This peaceful garden is hidden on the grounds of a spiritual organization founded by a pioneer yogi.

name: The Pasadena Veloway
description: A Bicycle Path in the Sky.

name: Triforium
description: A visionary, futuristic sculpture in Los Angeles that never met its extraterrestrial potential.

name: Nethercutt Collection and Museum
description: Rare collectibles from automated musical instruments to 250 unique automobiles.

name: Nike Missile Control Site LA-96
description: A Cold War site complete with rusty structures, hiking trails, and beautiful views.

name: River Under the First Bridge
description: An iconic location along the Los Angeles River where countless movie scenes are filmed.

name: Lookout Mountain Airforce Station
description: Secret US military film studio in the Hollywood Hills.

name: The Munch Box
description: A 1950s Googie burger stand is now a Los Angeles landmark.

name: Ballerina Clown
description: A three-story, three-dimensional hobo clown in a tutu welcomes CVS shoppers.

name: Cilantro Mexican Grill
description: A classically trained chef makes beloved burritos inside a gas station.

name: Los Angeles Breakfast Club
description: Friendships have been forged over ham and eggs every Wednesday morning for a century.

name: Galápagos Tortoise Skeleton
description: This rare tortoise skeleton has been hanging around the downtown Los Angeles museum for over a century.

name: Capitol Records Building Morse Code
description: The blinking light atop the iconic landmark has been sending secret messages for decades.

name: Muscle Beach
description: An historic and oft-filmed spot for musclebound iron pumpers on the Pacific coast.

name: Stone Gates of Hollywood
description: These two monoliths have stood at the entrance to the neighborhood since the days it was called "Hollywoodland."

name: Camera Collection at the American Society of Cinematographers
description: Inside this clubhouse is a cinephile's paradise.

name: Brady Bunch Home
description: The exterior of this home was used for the hit television show, The Brady Bunch.

name: Tom House
description: Historic house-museum dedicated to the revolutionary gay graphic artist Tom of Finland.

name: William Andrews Clark Memorial Library
description: "The greatest unknown literary treasure in Los Angeles."

name: Norton Sales Inc.
description: Space program, pneumatics and more, bits and pieces, for movie sets or the common man.

name: Angelus Temple
description: Church and home-museum of a 1920s female revivalist preacher with a very sordid past.

name: Mel Brooks Handprint
description: There are lots of hands and feet stamped in the cement in front of Grauman’s Chinese Theater, but Mel left behind something extra.

name: Historic Orange Grove
description: The last orange grove of the San Fernando Valley is owned and protected by CSU Northridge.

name: Marciano Art Foundation
description: A long-vacant Scottish Rite temple in Hollywood has been converted into an art museum filled with Masonic relics.

name: Tempest Freerunning Academy
description: An academy where anyone can train in the exciting acrobatic sports of parkour and freerunning.

name: Jim Morrison Mural
description: Legendary frontman of the Doors looms in this giant mural by famed Venice muralist, Rip Cronk.

name: Richfield Tower's Art Deco Doors
description: Though the original building has been demolished, these massive bronze doors were saved.

name: Metropolitan Pit Stop
description: This vintage car parts store is also a museum devoted to a squat little gem of automotive history.

name: Fire Survivors of the Griffith Park Merry-Go-Round
description: Three vintage horses survived a 1976 inferno and found a home on a sister carousel.

name: LAX Theme Building
description: This quirky UFO-shaped curiosity is often mistaken for the airport’s control tower.

name: Room 8 the Cat Mural
description: Hand-crafted tributes to a stray cat named "Room 8" now cover this LA elementary school.

name: Hieroglyph Murals at the Egyptian Theatre
description: The decor of this Hollywood movie palace just barely predated the opening of King Tut's tomb.

name: Caioti Pizza Café
description: Pizza joint known for labor-inducing salad dressing.

name: Kim Thai Food
description: Find regional specialties from the Thai-Laotian border inside in a North Hollywood swap meet's food court.

name: Los Angeles Pet Memorial Park
description: Pay your respects to departed pets, both celebrity and otherwise.

name: Aetherius Society's American Headquarters
description: "Prepare yourself! You are to become the voice of Interplanetary Parliament."

name: High Tower Elevator
description: Instead of hoofing it up steep stairs, residents can hitch a ride on this private lift.

name: Free Little Library (#0924)
description: A free library to stumble across, one of many placed worldwide if you know where to look.

name: Odd Fellows Cemetery
description: A Los Angeles cemetery that was once at the center of a Supreme Court dispute.

name: Mr. Ed's Home
description: A classic American barn that was the home of a classic American television star.

name: Old Trapper's Lodge Statues
description: A wild west scene starring life-sized wooden statues hides in the trees behind Pierce College.

name: Lincoln Heights Jail
description: Once the central jail for Los Angeles, this building housed inmates like Al Capone, those arrested from the Zoot Suit and Watts Riots, and individuals imprisoned for their sexuality.

name: Mt. Hollywood Tunnel
description: This nondescript tunnel has been featured in a number of iconic movies, from “Roger Rabbit” to “Back to the Future.”

name: Biddy Mason Memorial Park
description: A beautiful memorial tells the story of a formerly enslaved woman whose kindness shaped a burgeoning city.

name: Venice Beach Poet's Monument
description: The words of the neighborhood's most famous poets adorn four concrete walls.

name: ‘Corporate Head’
description: This sculpture presents commentary on a life in pursuit of profits.

name: Fort Macarthur Museum
description: A historic fort commemorating the United States' naval history.

name: Reggie The Alligator
description: Raised illegally, Reggie now lives at the Los Angeles Zoo after authorities spent years tracking him down.

name: Touch of Venice Mural
description: A black and white mural stands out from its psychedelic surroundings.

name: Autry National Center
description: Wild West history in Hollywood style.

name: Table 31 at Tam O’Shanter
description: Sit at the "Disney table,” where Walt and his team of "imagineers" once dined on the regular.

name: The African American Fire Fighter Museum
description: Celebrating the accomplishments of Black firefighters in Los Angeles.

name: Cardiff Tower
description: This "synagogue" is not what it seems to be.

name: The LAPD Kōban
description: Common all over Japan, kōbans are small community police boxes, and now one is guarding one of L.A.’s megamalls.

name: Crossroads of the World
description: A few iconic structures are all that remain of America’s first outdoor shopping mall.

name: Fine Arts Building Atrium
description: A Romanesque sanctuary of calm amid the bustle of downtown Los Angeles.

name: Los Angeles Fire Department Museum
description: Formerly Fire Station No. 27, the museum is a hidden gem in the heart of Hollywood.

name: 'América Tropical'
description: One of Los Angeles's first murals once vibrantly depicted the dangers of United States imperialism.

name: Vineyard Station Trolley Tragedy
description: A lovely summer day by the sea becomes a nightmare.

name: Gower Gulch
description: The mall's looks were inspired by the cowboy extras who used to meet up—and cause real trouble—at this Hollywood hub.

name: Eagle Rock
description: The neighborhood of Eagle Rock is named after a gigantic boulder with a shadowy surprise.

name: King George Hotel
description: Where a great scandal unfolded with a famous female revivalist at its center.

name: The Many Plaques of Buster Keaton Studios
description: The silent film master's legacy commemorated in all the right and wrong places.

name: Whimsical Metal Sculptures on Palms and Marco Place
description: Perched high atop telephone poles is an uncanny outdoor art gallery that greets passersby on their commute.

name: Lady Liberty Building
description: A 50-foot tile mural mosaic for the 100-year anniversary of the Statue of Liberty.

name: Beyond Baroque
description: Founded by a high school teacher, this poetry magazine became a center for all things literary.

name: Kinney-Tabor House
description: A gift from Abbot Kinney and a little bit of piece of Black history in Venice Beach.

name: Lovell Health House
description: The first all glass and steel residence in the United States.

name: Neutra VDL Studio and Residences
description: This house turned museum chronicles the life and work of Richard Neutra.

name: Declaration Sculpture
description: 60 feet of towering steel balances gracefully.

name: Peano's Faces of Venice Beach
description: The real faces of Venice beach captured on Romanesque columns.

name: Travel Town Museum
description: A miniature train theme park, free to the public, laying at the base of the Santa Monica Mountains.

name: Doheny Mansion
description: Built in 1899, this luxurious home was part of one of the first gated communities in Los Angeles.

name: Traffic Circle Gondola
description: A lonely Gondola stuck on a traffic circle sits in a spot where it once would have floated down the Grand Canal.

name: Parkman Murals & Mosaics
description: These artworks take you traveling to mythical medieval Europe, the politicized grape fields of 1960s California, and deep below sea level.

name: Chase Mosaic Movie Montages
description: This Chase branch is an homage to Old Hollywood.

name: Bob Hope Grave and Memorial Garden
description: The final resting place of one of Hollywood’s most prolific and talented actors is on the grounds of a California Mission.

name: Waldorf Hotel Apartments
description: Apartment building was once posh playground to the Hollywood elite.

name: 'The History of World Commerce'
description: A relief that wraps around the concourse of Los Angeles's World Trade Center marks one of the city's failed urban design plans.

name: Mel Blanc’s Grave
description: The man who voiced Bugs Bunny, Daffy Duck, Porky Pig, and more has a headstone reading, "That's all, folks!"

name: Venice Post Office's Abbot Kinney Mural
description: An homage to the man who built California's version of Venice.

name: Site of Charlie Chaplin Studios
description: The Hollywood home of Kermit the Frog and Yoda used to be Charlie Chaplin's film studio.

name: The (W)rapper
description: This jagged colossus of a building is mostly held together by an enormous concrete exoskeleton.

name: 'Four Great Eras of California History'
description: Inside this library, the history of Los Angeles is retold through a mural.

name: Naval and Marine Corps Reserve Training Center
description: Opened as part of the New Deal, the training facility now features World War II-era structures and a pylon that once stood at the World Trade Center.

name: The Los Angeles Times Bombing Memorial
description: This grand memorial remembers the 20 members of the Los Angeles Times killed when the office was bombed.

name: José de San Martín Bust
description: A forgotten tribute to a South American hero hidden in a cluster of traffic islands.

name: Griffith J. Griffith Statue
description: Although he donated over 3,000 acres to Los Angles, Griffith's life was marred by controversy.

name: Ilé
description: Hidden in a Hollywood apartment, this restaurant takes diners on a gastronomic journey through West Africa.

name: Circus Liquor Clown
description: This iconic neon jester is a local landmark that you might recognize from his cameos in film and television.

name: Chaplin Keaton Lloyd Alley
description: The three biggest silent film stars once shot iconic scenes here.

name: Hollywood Bowl Museum
description: This museum is a music lover's introduction to the iconic venue.

name: 'Dan the Miner'
description: Murder, searching for gold, and being cut in half—the memorial statue of Daniel O. McCarthy and the Pioneers of '49

name: Charlotte and Robert Disney House
description: This humble American Craftsman home is where the Disney brothers’ animation business took flight in 1923.

name: The Palms Rooftop Hot Dog
description: This beloved, whimsical weiner topped an eclectic variety of Los Angeles buildings, including a Thai restaurant and an advertising agency.

name: The Bust of Juan Bautista De Anza
description: Anza led the first Spanish settlers from Mexico to Monterey, California in the 1770s.

name: Campo de Cahuenga Memorial Park
description: Overshadowed by the theme park opposite it, this small museum explores a major moment in American history.

name: Cahuenga Building
description: This bank is widely believed to be the inspiration for the offices of Philip Marlowe, the hardboiled detective featured in a number of Raymond Chandler's stories.

name: Vista Theatre
description: The historic single-screen movie theater is one of only four cinemas in the world to screen films in VistaVison format.

name: The Good Ship Grace
description: This ship-shape Streamline Moderne building was once the headquarters of a nautically inclined radio preacher.

name: Casa de Cadillac
description: Glass-walled, mid-century modern showroom for cars, still in operation.

name: Underground Tunnels of Los Angeles
description: During prohibition, corrupt city officials ran drinking dens under the streets of Downtown Los Angeles.

name: Clifton's Cafeteria
description: Giant Redwood-themed cafeteria operates under the golden rule, "Dine Free Unless Delighted."

name: California Institute of Abnormalarts (CIA)
description: Venue for strange sideshow displays and musical acts.

name: Necromance
description: Medical and zoological curiosities for sale.

name: Café Jack
description: A "Titanic" superfan changed his name to Jack and set up this strange shrine to the blockbuster, complete with Korean food and Tarot readings.

name: Cactus Store
description: The neighborhood shop is a sort of tiny cactus museum full of exotic specimens you can take home.

name: The Silent Movie Theater
description: The last of its kind in the country.

name: Skeletons in the Closet
description: Gift shop offering toe tags and chalk body beach towels tucked into a coroner's office.

name: The Bunny Museum
description: "The Hoppiest Place in the World" holds the world’s largest collection of bunny memorabilia and is a singular testament of a couple's devotion to all things Leporidae.

name: Chandelier Tree
description: Dozens of lights dangle from the branches of what may be the brightest tree in Los Angeles.

name: Dearly Departed Museum
description: A macabre tour of Hollywood’s deadly history.

name: Cakeland.LA
description: A cake-themed art installation tucked away in historic Chinatown.

name: Los Angeles' Museum of Broken Relationships
description: Where the leftovers of love affairs can live on forever.

name: Velveteria
description: The art and history of the velvet painting are on full display in this Los Angeles museum.

name: Venice Beach Freakshow
description: The largest collection of two headed animals in the world.

name: Snow White Cafe
description: This Hollywood dive houses a mural painted by the artists who animated its namesake film.

name: Everything Is Terrible
description: This store is dedicated to unique found footage and the strangeness of pre-internet pop-culture.

name: Institute For Figuring
description: This hands-on math museum believes in turning concepts into constructs.

name: almighty Opp
description: A monthly street "service" of handmade puppets and live original puppets, whose devotees profess has the power to change lives.

name: Caine's Arcade
description: A 9 year-old boy’s elaborate cardboard arcade in his father’s used auto parts store.

name: Synchronicity Space
description: An enchanted arts space which assumes infinite identities.

name: The Babylon Court
description: This centerpiece of the Hollywood & Highland shopping center is a recreation of a long-lost movie set.

name: Youngwood Court
description: Iconic Los Angeles residence famous for its 19 statues of Michelangelo's David.

name: Danny's Deli
description: Authentic gondola hanging from the ceiling, notable mural in the back room.

name: Robinson's Liberty Bells
description: Large but largely ignored, these 42 life-size Liberty Bell replicas were a peculiar, unexplained feature on the facade of a Macy's department store.

name: Farmacia Y Botanica Million Dollar
description: Once a purveyor of Santeria supplies, religious products , and household sundries, now closed.

name: Giant Scissors
description: Giant scissors seemingly stabbed into the side of a hair salon leave no question about the vocation of the owners.

name: The Happy Foot/Sad Foot Sign
description: A cult following celebrates the legend that belies this curious podiatrists' office sign which has worked its way into song, literature, animation, and the visual arts.

name: Dennis Hopper Mural
description: A tribute to long time Venice Beach resident, artist and actor Dennis Hopper.

name: My Brother's Bar-B-Q Cow
description: A cow in the road might subvert the flow of traffic but this one's above it all, quite literally.

name: Wells Fargo History Museum
description: Hidden in one of the skyscrapers on Bunker Hill, this museum features an 1895 stagecoach and peers into California’s gold-lined history.

name: 'Remnal of a Senging Chave'
description: Behind legendary Meltdown Comics is a marker for an engineered musical roadway of the Tehachapi, the great road builders of Kcymaerxthaere.
